remediation: Relax the feature-cycle-state guards that sit UPSTREAM of cycle-030's already-correct bug-release bridge so a confirmed bug-fix-only cycle (passing .doti/bugs/<id>/test.json, NO .doti/cycle-state.json) is releasable as patch without fabricating a numbered feature cycle. (1) CycleService.GetReleaseTrain (CycleService.ReleaseTrain.cs:10-12): when _store.Read() is null, do NOT throw — fall through to BuildReleaseTrain(state:null) with the feature-half (CompletionRecordsForRelease/featureMembers) empty while still consulting _bugReleaseMembers(_repositoryRoot) (:48, which never needed state); make state CycleState? + null-guard CompletionRecordsForRelease + ReleaseTrainDriftDetector.Detect. Same for MarkReleaseTrainReleased (:19-21) — but BugReleaseGit.IsReleased self-maintains shipped-status via git-tag-reachability (BugReleaseGit.cs:9-12,31-47), so the release tag alone likely suffices (no new marker write); confirm in-cycle. This fixes BOTH gate run --profile release (GateRunner.cs:93) and hx release (LocalReleaseService.cs:39-40), which both throw via GetReleaseTrain. (2) doti cycle stamp --stage release (ResolveStampFeature, CycleService.Stamp.cs:194-198): a bug-only release should NOT require a feature stamp — the release-train validity (CycleService.Check release-train + GetReleaseTrain, now fixed) is the gate; the stamp is a feature-cycle concept (doti-bug runs outside the numbered cycle). Prefer shape (b): gate --profile release + hx release succeed for a bug-only repo with NO release stamp; assert the other shape isn't silently half-working. (3) Optional: a discoverable hx doti bug close wrapping gate+release (UX, not root). Patch-default (LocalReleaseVersionPolicy.cs:14-15) already correct. Tests (deterministic seam, NEVER write a CycleState): bug-only repo (passing test.json) -> GetReleaseTrain valid + releasable as patch; no-bug+no-cycle -> still fails closed; the 4 existing ReleaseTrainBridgeTests pass unchanged; post-tag round-trip excludes the released bug.
summary: The release path (CycleService.GetReleaseTrain, used by gate --profile release + hx release) throws on a null .doti/cycle-state.json BEFORE the 030 bug-release bridge runs, so a bug-fix-only repo (only a /doti-bug mini-cycle, never a numbered feature cycle) cannot release a patch. Relax the guard to admit the no-cycle-state bug-only case the bridge already handles; a bug-only release needs no feature stamp.
